title: n8n Git node argument injection via pathsToAdd enables arbitrary file read
description: The n8n Git node 'add' operation takes the user-supplied 'pathsToAdd' workflow parameter, splits it on commas, and passes the resulting array directly as arguments to the underlying git CLI via simple-git's git.add(). Because the user-controlled tokens are placed into the git argv with no '--' end-of-options separator and no validation, an authenticated workflow author can inject git command-line flags (CWE-88 argument injection) instead of plain paths. By injecting flags/pathspecs that cause git to dereference or stage attacker-chosen locations, an attacker can read arbitrary files from the n8n server, potentially leading to full compromise. The same unsafe pattern also applied to the commit operation's pathsToAdd handling.
